prompt:
Feature ticket for the quote-request form. formcodec.ts is the serializer behind draft autosave and it has been in production for a quarter: collectPairs(fields) decides what a form submits (document order, disabled fields and empty names skipped, checkbox/radio only when checked, multi-select one pair per selected option, single select first selected or nothing) and encodePairs(pairs) writes application/x-www-form-urlencoded (space as +, the A-Za-z0-9*-._ alphabet literal, everything else uppercase %XX utf-8 bytes). serialize is the composition. That half is DONE — the existing-behavior tests in test_formcodec.ts pass today and must stay green; don't reshuffle working code for style points.

The feature: restore a saved draft back into the form. Drafts are stored as the exact string serialize produced, so we need the decode direction in the same module:

- parse(encoded: string): Array<[string, string]> — split on '&' (empty segments between separators are skipped; empty input gives []), split each segment on the FIRST '=' only (no '=' means empty value; a segment may also start with '=' and keep its empty name), then decode both halves: '+' is a space, %XX takes either hex case, decoded bytes are utf-8. Order and duplicate names are preserved exactly — repeated names are how multi-selects and repeated fields serialize, so parse returns pairs, not an object. This is a BODY codec: '?' and other literals have no special meaning and pass through.
- export class FormDecodeError extends Error — thrown for undecodable input: '%' not followed by two hex digits (truncated or non-hex), and byte sequences that are not valid utf-8. Strict, not lenient: a draft that doesn't decode is corrupt and the caller wants to know, not to get mojibake into the form.

The laws the tests pin down: parse(serialize(fields)) deep-equals collectPairs(fields) for any form model, and encodePairs(parse(s)) === s for any canonically encoded s. Scope guard: drafts never contain file uploads, so this stays application/x-www-form-urlencoded only — no multipart handling of any kind.

Acceptance is node --test test_formcodec.ts all green (the runner is plain node 26 type stripping — keep the module erasable-syntax only). The test file is read-only.
